title: Today's Markets Brief: Why did Micron stock fall 13% on Friday and should you buy the dip?
summary: A quick look at the market news, focusing on Micron's stock drop and SpaceX's potential IPO.

body.text: Next up, I'm seeing some interesting commentary from Yardeni Research about SpaceX's potential IPO. The question is: could SpaceX be the next trillion-dollar listing? It's a pretty lofty goal, but not entirely out of the realm of possibility. With companies like Tesla and Amazon pushing the boundaries of valuation, it's not hard to imagine SpaceX following suit. The article notes that SpaceX's recent funding rounds have valued the company at over $100 billion, so it's definitely on the radar. I'm curious to see how this one plays out - space is a pretty exciting (and expensive) frontier.

The other thing that's got me thinking is just how quickly the market can shift. One day, Micron's stock is down 13%, and the next, everyone's wondering if it's a buying opportunity. It's a good reminder to stay on top of market news and not get too caught up in the daily fluctuations.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
